chunk_summary: This section discusses outpatient recovery criteria, maximum treatment duration, and referral protocols for non-responding cases in the community-based management of severe acute malnutrition (SAM) in India.
learning_objectives: Understand the outpatient recovery criteria for SAM in India.
Identify the maximum duration of outpatient treatment before referral for non-response.
Learn about referral protocols for non-responding SAM cases in community-based programs.
key_facts: Outpatient recovery criteria for SAM include achieving a weight-for-height z-score (WHZ) ≥ -2 or mid-upper arm circumference (MUAC) ≥ 12.5 cm.
Maximum duration of outpatient treatment is typically 8-12 weeks, after which non-responding cases are referred to inpatient care.
Non-response is defined as failure to gain weight or meet recovery criteria within the specified treatment duration.
Community-based management of SAM is endorsed by WHO, UNICEF, and other global organizations.
India's CMAM guidelines emphasize early detection and treatment of SAM to reduce mortality rates.
reference_quote: "Non-response is defined as failure to gain weight or meet recovery criteria within the specified treatment duration." (Source: WHO, WFP, UNSCN, and UNICEF Joint Statement, 2007)
document_key_topics: Referral protocols for non-responding SAM cases
